records.title: Home Purchase Loans
records.preview: A useful purchase plan connects the home price with cash to close, the full monthly housing obligation, post-closing reserves, and the time you expect to own the property. Loan programs can structure those costs differently, so compare written scenarios for the same home and borrower facts before deciding.

records.title: Home Equity / HELOC
records.preview: Home equity borrowing places an additional obligation against the home. A home equity loan generally provides a lump sum, while a HELOC provides reusable access during a draw period under its agreement. Compare funding needs, rate structure, fees, repayment rules, lien position, and the effect on future plans.

records.title: Cash-Out Refinance
records.preview: A cash-out refinance replaces the current first mortgage with a larger new loan and provides eligible proceeds after existing liens and transaction costs are addressed. It changes the financing on the entire mortgage balance, so compare the new rate, term, payment, costs, equity, and use of funds with alternatives.

records.title: Conventional Loans
records.preview: Conventional mortgages are not insured or guaranteed by FHA, VA, or USDA. Some conform to standards used by Fannie Mae or Freddie Mac, while others do not. A productive comparison focuses on the applicable loan limit, borrower and property review, mortgage-insurance treatment, pricing, and total cost.

records.title: FHA Loans
records.preview: FHA-insured mortgages are made by approved private lenders and follow federal program rules. They may offer a useful purchase or refinance path, but the down payment is only one part of the decision. Mortgage insurance, county loan limits, property review, lender underwriting, and the complete written offer all matter.

records.title: Jumbo Loans
records.preview: A jumbo loan is generally a conventional mortgage above the applicable conforming loan limit. Jumbo products are less standardized across lenders, so documentation, reserves, appraisal practices, property eligibility, and pricing can vary. Borrowers benefit from comparing complete written offers rather than relying on...

records.title: VA Loans
records.preview: VA-backed loans can provide a distinct financing path for eligible Veterans, service members, and certain surviving spouses. A Certificate of Eligibility documents benefit information, but the lender still reviews the borrower and property. Entitlement, funding-fee treatment, value, closing costs, and occupancy belong...

records.title: Refinance
records.preview: Refinancing replaces an existing mortgage with a new loan. The decision should begin with a defined goal and a side-by-side comparison of the current loan and proposed loan, including closing costs, term, payment, total interest, and the time required to recover upfront expense.
